summary: 6 concrete product references surfaced from 25 qualifying HN stories, with broad coverage across art tools, travel resources, AI dev tools, and infra.

items.name: Google Arts & Culture
items.summary: Google's art-and-museum platform is a handy companion for exploring ultra-high-resolution artwork and exhibits.
items.why_included: A commenter explicitly recommended it as a great resource for seeing and learning about art around the world.
items.evidence: In the Guernica gigapixel thread, a commenter said artsandculture.google.com is 'a great resource to see and learn about art around the world.'

items.name: OpenSeadragon
items.summary: An open-source deep-zoom viewer for tiled images, maps, and other high-resolution visuals.
items.why_included: The thread called it an under-appreciated tool and pointed to it as the right building block for tiled-image viewers.
items.evidence: A commenter said someone forked OpenSeadragon and called it 'such an under-appreciated tool,' adding that VIPS can handle tile generation.

items.name: Wikivoyage
items.source_story_title: guide.world: A compendium of travel guides
items.summary: A community-edited travel guide wiki that works well as a living alternative to static trip notes.
items.why_included: A commenter suggested it as the wiki version worth using and updating during trip research.
items.evidence: One commenter wrote, 'For the wiki version, you have Wikivoyage... It can be a bit outdated, but then you just update it as you do your research.'

items.name: SBB Mobile
items.source_story_title: guide.world: A compendium of travel guides
items.summary: Swiss Federal Railways' trip-planning app for routes, tickets, and smooth point-to-point travel.
items.why_included: The thread praised it as a polished, stress-free companion for getting around Switzerland.
items.evidence: A commenter said the SBB Mobile app was 'god-sent' and 'so well made,' noting that its design system makes travel up and down the country stress-free.

items.name: Codex
items.summary: OpenAI's coding agent was cited as a durable fallback for longer-running development tasks.
items.why_included: Commenters framed it as a more thorough alternative in the Claude ecosystem debate.
items.evidence: In the Claude Code Routines thread, one commenter said they were finding Codex 'more thorough' on the high plan, and another named OpenCode/Codex as the first fallback if Anthropic goes bad.

items.name: OpenSSL 4.0.0
items.summary: The new major OpenSSL release drew interest for long-awaited TLS feature work.
items.why_included: A top comment reacted positively to one of the new protocol features, marking the release as noteworthy.
items.evidence: A commenter celebrated 'Finally encrypted client hello support ο/,' showing enthusiasm for the new OpenSSL release.
